channel.title: drivenemo novels
channel.description: Illustrated original web novels with scene art and looping action clips.

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 1: The Man in the Cage
item.content:encoded: Jophery Brown had both hands on the gate when the crate …

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 2: Temporary Badge
item.content:encoded: It had been nine days since Jophery Brown died when Mateo Valverde signed the temporary contract…

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 3: The Places Guests Don't See
item.content:encoded:
By the fifth day, Mateo Valverde knew the park by smell.

The visitor center smelled of new stone, cold air, and ex…

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 4: Five-Minute Closure
item.content:encoded:
At 6:31 p.m., the last shuttle call came through the service speakers.

The speaker above the …

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 5: Jophery Brown
item.content:encoded:
Mateo Valverde did not move for six seconds.

He knew because the clock above the clean lockers still w…

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 6: Profile Not Found
item.content:encoded:
Mateo Valverde ran the wrong way first.

That was what saved him from believing the park still …

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 7: Dead Man's Access
item.content:encoded:
Mateo Valverde stayed at the fence until the boat light became the same color as the rain.

Then he made himself coun…

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 8: The Last Shuttle
item.content:encoded:
The breath came from low down.

Mateo did not move his feet. Moving feet made rubber squeak on wet concrete. He had learned that in the rapt…

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 9: Return to Sender
item.content:encoded:
Mateo slid under the shuttle bench.

It was not bravery. It was not a plan. It was the only place his body found before the step…

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 10: The Park Remembers the Dead
item.content:encoded: The staff break room was behind the maintenance office, past a soda machine with no power and a door that opened for Jophery without asking…

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 11: Rex Weather
item.content:encoded:
The safest route on Jophery’s sheet ran toward the sound.

Mateo did not know what made the sound. Not at first. The building did not shake like …

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 12: Utility Level B
item.content:encoded:
Mateo got out through the floor.

That was not a thing the safety signs admitted a person could do. The safety signs preferred stairs, mu…

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 13: Kitchen Light
item.content:encoded:
The door opened because a forgotten reader remembered him.

Mateo stood with his hand still on his temporary badge and watched the green light as if…

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 14: Not My Rescue
item.content:encoded:
Mateo did not run toward the guest stairs.

He hated that later.

At the time, he saw three things: the freezer reflection, the service door t…

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 15: Compy Work
item.content:encoded:
The little ones came through the trash room.

Mateo heard them before he saw them. Not footsteps. Too light for that. Scratches. P…

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 16: Quarantine Route
item.content:encoded:
The quarantine route did not start with a door.

It started with a smell.

Not rot. Not meat. Not disinfectant. Something medicinal u…

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 17: Counting Error
item.content:encoded:
Mateo held the vent grille in place with the mop handle while Rosa turned the screws back in by hand.

The thing in the vent pushed once....

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 18: Raptor Silence
item.content:encoded:
The raptor service corridor had gone quiet.

Mateo had never heard it quiet before.

Even empty, the place had made noises. Fans. Drains. dist…

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 19: Full Reboot
item.content:encoded:
The door opened inward.

Mateo fell through it because he had been leaning into the reader, expecting failure. Rosa fell afte…

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 20: After the Click
item.content:encoded:
After the click, the park woke in pieces.

Not the right pieces.

Fans came on in rooms with open doors. Locks cycled in empty halls. Freezer alarms res…

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 21: Kitchen Without Children
item.content:encoded:
The kitchen had no children in it.

That was the first mercy.

It had everything children left behind.

Muddy prints on tile. A ladle under the s…

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 22: North Dock Promise
item.content:encoded:
North Dock was not on the guest map.

It was on three bad maps and one good rumor.

Jophery’s route sheet showed a service road broken by a creek cro…

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 23: The Wrong Dead Man
item.content:encoded:
Mateo knew the right answer.

That was the problem.

He looked at Rosa. She had one hand on the rope around Luis and one hand on the pipe rail. S…

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 24: Using the Park
item.content:encoded:
Mateo stopped being polite to the park.

That was the difference.

Before North Dock, he had asked doors to open. He had asked radios to believe him. He had ask…

item.title: Jurassic Left Behind: Chapter 25: Left Behind
item.content:encoded:
The launch access reader did not care that Mateo was alive.

Red.

He tried Tomás’s badge.

Red.

Jophery’s.

Red.

Three cards. Three little death…

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 1: The Bite at Bahía Anasco
item.content:encoded: It had been four hours since the helicopter set down on the sand below the clinic....

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 2: The Lizard at Cabo Blanco
item.content:encoded:
It had been a hundred and forty miles since Mike Bowman had said the word paradise to his wife in a way that meant he was tired of saying it.

![A long empty Costa Rican beach at sunrise, white sand running to a green headland, a single rented Land Rover parked at the tree line, three small footpr…

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 3: A Drawing in Crayon
item.content:encoded:
It had been two weeks since the lizard at Cabo Blanco when Dr. Richard Stone, of the Columbia University Tropical Diseases Laboratory, opened the manila envelope from the Clínica Santa María in Puntarenas, Costa Rica.

![A small office at the Columbia Tropical Diseases Lab: a grey steel desk, a sing…

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 4: The Dart and the Howler
item.content:encoded: It had been nine days since Alice Levin had said Procompsognathus in Richard Stone’s office in New York when Marty Guitierrez, of the Carara Biological Station on the Costa Rican mainland, took the dart pistol out of the back of his Land Rover and began to walk in a slow careful arc through the tr…

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 5: The Old Man's Pitch
item.content:encoded: It had been three days since the call from Marty Guitierrez when John Hammond, of InGen Corporation, walked into the thirty-fourth-floor conference room of Cowan, Swain & Ross at One Embarcadero in San Francisco, set a small portable cage on the polished walnut table, lifted the cloth from the cage …

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 6: The Other Bidder
item.content:encoded:
It was raining on Cupertino the morning the Biosyn board of directors voted, by a show of hands, to steal what John Hammond had built.

![A long fluorescent-lit boardroom on the second floor of a low corporate building in Cupertino, six men in dark suits seated around a glass-topped table, a single …

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 7: The Shaving-Cream Can
item.content:encoded: It had been six days since the Biosyn boardroom when Dennis Nedry, of San Mateo, walked across the Saturday-morning food court of San Francisco International Airport at twenty minutes after eight in the morning, bought a cup of coffee at the kiosk between the bagel place and the pretzel place, sat d…

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 8: The Snakewater Eggshell
item.content:encoded:
It had been five weeks since the start of the Snakewater season when Alan Grant set his palm flat against the warm shaly hillside and felt, through the rock, the muffled thump of the sonic device the students up the slope called Thumper.

Alan Grant’s palm was the palm of a paleontologist who had sp…

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 9: The Helicopter Through the Cloud
item.content:encoded: It had been nineteen hours since the rotor wash off the InGen Bell JetRanger had flattened the bunchgrass at the Choteau airstrip when Alan Grant, of the University of Denver, fastened the four-point harness over the chest of his field shirt in the rear cabin of a second helicopter, this one a large…

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 10: Welcome to Jurassic Park
item.content:encoded: It had been forty minutes since the trumpeting of the apatosaurs above the palms when Ellie Sattler, of the University of Denver, sat down in a leather seat in the small auditorium of the Isla Nublar visitor center and felt, in the cool conditioned air on the back of her neck, the small unlocatable …

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 11: Two Hundred and Thirty-Eight
item.content:encoded: It had been twenty minutes since the infant Velociraptor had blinked once at Ellie Sattler in the nursery when Donald Gennaro, of Cowan, Swain & Ross, stepped through the security door of the Isla Nublar control room and, for the first time since the Sikorsky had set down on the helipad in the cloud…

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 12: The Sick Stegosaur
item.content:encoded: It had been ninety minutes since the species count on the control-room screen had stopped at one thousand nine hundred and thirty-two when Ellie Sattler, in the lead Land Cruiser of the standard tour route, set her left palm flat against the inside of the door and felt, through the door, the slow ro…

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 13: The Field with No Mothers
item.content:encoded: It had been twelve minutes since Gerry Harding’s jeep had pulled away across the meadow when Alan Grant, of the University of Denver, who had not got back into the lead Land Cruiser with the others, who had instead said give me five minutes and waited until Ellie Sattler had walked across the wet …

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 14: Two Hundred and Ninety-Two
item.content:encoded: It had been thirty minutes since Alan Grant had finished the count of the procompsognathid hatchlings on the slope above the maintenance road and had gotten back into the Land Cruiser, and in that thirty minutes the air over the central highland of Isla Nublar had thickened, the way air thickens bef…

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 15: The Wrong Turn
item.content:encoded:
Dennis Nedry’s hands were on the keyboard at six twenty-two in the evening.

The keyboard was the operator keyboard at the second console of the control room, the small one in the corner Ray Arnold did not like to use because the swivel chair under it was broken, and the small swivel chair was, in t…

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 16: The Concrete Barrier
item.content:encoded:
The animal was forty feet away.

The animal was, by the small careful audit Dennis Nedry was running on it from a foot from the door handle of the Jeep, ten feet tall at the shoulder, and the body of the animal was yellow with black spots and the throat of the animal was a pale color Dennis Nedry co…

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 17: The Goat Tether
item.content:encoded:
It had been twelve minutes since the two Toyota Land Cruisers, running on the buried induction cable that fed power to their drive wheels along the central scenic loop of Jurassic Park, had stopped on the rise of the hill above the tyrannosaur paddock.

The cars stopped together.

The cars stopped, …

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 18: The Rex Breaks the Wire
item.content:encoded:
It had been forty-one minutes since the two Toyota Land Cruisers had stopped on the road north of the goat field with no white painted line under their bumpers and no recorded voice on the speakers and no reply on the dashboard radio.

The rain came down on the roofs of the cars in the slow heavy wa…

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 19: The Boy in the Tree
item.content:encoded:
Tim Murphy held the branch with both hands and did not let go.

The branch was the second branch up from the place where the Land Cruiser had stopped, which was no longer a place because the Land Cruiser had been picked up by the Tyrannosaurus rex and lifted six meters into the air and dropped, and …

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 20: The River and the Swimmer
item.content:encoded:
The raft pushed out from the rock at four-eleven in the morning when the river current took the rubber bow and turned it sideways and Alan Grant, who had pushed off with one foot in the shallow water and the other knee on the deck, fell forward and caught himself on his palms.

Lex Murphy did not la…

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 21: The Aviary Lodge
item.content:encoded:
Lex Murphy stepped onto the wet planks of the aviary dock with the baseball glove tucked under her left arm and her right hand in the back pocket of Alan Grant’s khaki field shirt.

The shirt was not hers.

The shirt had been put on her, over her wet sundress, by Alan Grant ten minutes earlier when …

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 22: Dawn at Building Four
item.content:encoded: It had been six hours since the raft had pushed off from the rock above the river when the first sun came up over the ridge behind the Pteratops Lodge and put a long flat orange light through the smashed picture window of the lodge dining room and onto the back of a small gray-brown animal the size …

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 23: The Maintenance Shed
item.content:encoded: It had been five hours since the auxiliary generator had begun running, and John Arnold, of Cambridge, Massachusetts, who held a doctorate in electrical engineering and had been the chief engineer of Jurassic Park for eleven months, had not, in those five hours, thought to look at the small green re…

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 24: Bait at the Lodge Fence
item.content:encoded: It had been four minutes since the shoulder launcher had gone off behind the visitor center when Ellie Sattler, of Berkeley, California, who had walked across cattle pasture in three countries and had once put her hand into a fer-de-lance den on a bet, stood inside the front door of the Safari Lodge…

item.title: Jurassic Awakening: Chapter 25: The Touchscreen Child
item.content:encoded:
Tim Murphy was eleven years old.

Tim Murphy had at home in Chappaqua, New York, a Commodore 64 with a flickering composite monitor, an Apple IIe in his school’s library that he was allowed on for forty minutes each Friday, a paperback copy of Hackers by Steven Levy that he had read three times, a…
